evidence.claim: If any operation within the transaction fails, all previous operations must be rolled back.
evidence.fact: Each withdrawal is processed as an all-or-nothing operation; if any single step fails, all previous changes are undone completely.
evidence.user_story_span: If any operation within the transaction fails, all previous operations must be rolled back.
evidence.fact_span: if any single step fails, all previous changes are undone completely.

evidence.claim: The transaction must be either fully committed or fully rolled back with no partial state changes.
evidence.fact: Each withdrawal is processed as an all-or-nothing operation; if any single step fails, all previous changes are undone completely.
evidence.user_story_span: The transaction should be either fully committed or fully rolled back with no partial state changes.
evidence.fact_span: if any single step fails, all previous changes are undone completely.

evidence.claim: When one or more operations fail, no changes from the transaction are persisted.
evidence.fact: Each withdrawal is processed as an all-or-nothing operation; if any single step fails, all previous changes are undone completely.
evidence.fact_span: if any single step fails, all previous changes are undone completely.

evidence.claim: The user can create a transaction with multiple operations.
evidence.fact: Suspicious withdrawals are tagged in the system, alerting administrators to review them for possible fraud.

evidence.claim: When one or more operations fail, the transaction is rolled back.
evidence.fact: The system performs the entire withdrawal sequence in a single unit, and upon any error, automatically reverses every modification made.
evidence.fact_span: upon any error, automatically reverses every modification made.
